tweets.text: We are committed to building a diplomatic corps that represents America. As we mark #Pride Month, @StateDeptSpox shares some of the many contributions LGBTQI+ employees are making to advance U.S. interests and help American citizens. Visit https://t.co/CqRHMQ9GyE for more. https://t.co/0BKCQ8ujhy

On this International Day of Parliamentarism, the U.S. reaffirms our commitment to supporting parliamentarism to advance inclusive democracies globally. For more on our efforts, read the latest @StateDRL blog. https://t.co/e3ZmkjtP8F

When water is scarce, whether due to environmental factors or underdeveloped infrastructure, women & girls tend to lose economic & educational opportunities as water collection duties take increasingly more time. Learn more in @StateGWI’s latest DipNote: https://t.co/WRhpPWcstK

The U.S. is committed to advancing the human rights of LGBTQI+ persons around the world. Read more about our commitment to promoting and protecting the rights of LGBTQI+ people through our diplomacy and foreign assistance: https://t.co/qeGZJOign5. #PrideMonth

.@POTUS: “LGBTQI+ rights are human rights.” Everyone has a role to play in fighting hatred and discrimination. All people deserve respect and dignity and should be able to live without fear no matter who they are or whom they love. #Pride  https://t.co/qeGZJOign5 https://t.co/sOVOvUjj1b
.@POTUS: “We’re sending an unmistakable message…NATO is strong, united.” https://t.co/kuszKxYFXw  #NATOSummit https://t.co/LyvFkemYGr
.@POTUS: “We mean it when we say, ‘An attack against one is an attack against all — every inch’.” https://t.co/kuszKxYFXw #NATOSummit https://t.co/EMrSmvoa7f
The #NATOSummit is being held in Madrid, Spain this week. Learn more about the @NATO Summit’s key outcomes here: https://t.co/Bmh0PLQHlZ

This week at the #UNOceanConference, the United States is driving momentum for action to address the ocean-climate nexus, marine protected areas, IUU fishing, and plastic pollution. We all share the responsibility to protect our ocean.

.@POTUS:  Today, I’m announcing the United States will enhance our force posture in Europe and respond to the changed security environment, as well as strengthen our collective security. More: https://t.co/kuszKxYFXw

At the Global Health Security Conference, @SecBlinken reiterates why health security is national security and why an effective approach to pandemic prevention and preparedness requires an all-of-society effort.
.@SecBlinken: @NATO is emerging from this summit more united, more focused, and with more assets to deal with the multiplicity of challenges. #NATOSummit https://t.co/s0AlpgnVlw

Live now! @SecBlinken participates in a moderated discussion with Spanish Foreign Minister @JMAlbares on “@NATO After Madrid,” in Madrid, Spain. Also streaming on https://t.co/ByC4qHI0qr. https://t.co/qgKylA6NFL

In partnership with #COVAX, the United States donated an additional 4.1 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to Bangladesh. With this donation, we have now delivered over 68 million doses to the people of Bangladesh. Together, our efforts can help end the acute phase of the pandemic. https://t.co/MruU5AAp0k

This week, the United States donated 200,070 additional COVID-19 vaccine doses to Tajikistan through #COVAX. Our partnership with Tajikistan remains steadfast as we work to vaccinate more people and save lives around the world.

Atlantic hurricane season began this month. U.S. citizens who live in or must travel to storm-prone regions can register at https://t.co/1EuLSguti2 for updates. Help us contact you in emergencies. More: https://t.co/fb60Rl7Yel
.@POTUS arrives in Madrid 🇪🇸 for the @NATO Summit. #NATOSummit #MadridOTAN22
What is @NATO? @UnderSecPD Liz Allen gives us a quick refresher on what it stands for, why it's necessary, and how it helps achieve our foreign policy priorities. #WeAreNATO https://t.co/FeMxGdvUIY

Today in Madrid, 🇳🇿PM @jacindaarden addressed the first #Tech4Democracy Venture Day. This partnership w/ @IEuniversity encourages large-scale, sustainable worldwide development of democracy-affirming technologies as part of the @POTUS #SummitForDemocracy. https://t.co/hUFhL4EECA

.@VP on the @NATO Alliance: We are clear that the work that is to be done in response to Putin’s war includes standing strong within the Alliance to support the needs of our partners. https://t.co/4qrFmavphd

Since February, the United States has pledged $2.8 billion in humanitarian assistance to alleviate world hunger. We have also increased funding for food security development assistance by $760 million, in addition to over $1 billion planned this year. https://t.co/4JjSwhukdX https://t.co/r6030Pjhz8

The U.S., in partnership with #COVAX, is donating 299,520 more Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to the Philippines. To date, we have shared more than 33 million doses with the Philippines free of cost. https://t.co/cMYfAilZl3

.@POTUS: We need worldwide effort to invest in transformative clean energy projects to ensure that critical infrastructure is resilient to changing climate. https://t.co/U2zhM6L9t2 https://t.co/6NCUGWUUX1

The Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment will deliver game-changing projects to close the infrastructure gap in developing countries, strengthen the global economy and supply chains, and advance U.S. national security. https://t.co/tgj1qAF1U3

.@POTUS officially launches the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment: “This isn’t aid or charity; it’s an investment that will deliver returns for everyone, including the American people and the people of all our nations.” https://t.co/B3mlRD7BLs https://t.co/Ho12NpzjCn
.@SecBlinken: President Putin wanted to weaken @NATO with this war. Instead, NATO is stronger, more united, and on the cusp of enlarging. https://t.co/HTtIm8mcjU
The United States and our partners and allies are leading the world to ensure better sustainability and fisheries governance. Together we can fight against #IUUfishing. https://t.co/2kECYNyWaJ

This is the first in a series of grand challenges on democracy-affirming technologies that is part of @POTUS’s #SummitForDemocracy. It aims to galvanize worldwide innovation of technologies that offer the opportunity & potential to be used in ways that support democratic values.
FACT: Hundreds of historians and scholars have condemned the Russian government’s “cynical abuse of the term genocide, the memory of World War II and the Holocaust.” https://t.co/YiGdEe3N8U

This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX ​and the African Vaccine Acquisition Trust #AVAT to donate more than more 3.2 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to Mali, Nigeria, Seychelles, and Tanzania. Learn more about our vaccine donation efforts: https://t.co/meu9mciYdD https://t.co/siBAlvHoW9

President Putin is stopping food from being shipped and aggressively using his propaganda machine to deflect or distort responsibility because he hopes it’ll get the world to give in to him and end the sanctions. In other words, quite simply put, it’s blackmail. https://t.co/B2heUVIO39

In our ongoing Spotlight on the War in Ukraine in collaboration with @cabinetofficeuk, Democracy Champion Tomas shares how fragile democracy is and why it is important for citizens to continue to uphold it to protect our liberties. #DemocracyAllies #StandWithUkraine https://t.co/oEhZDSIzvX

At Schloss Elmau in Germany, @POTUS, @SecBlinken, and their @G7 counterparts will discuss a range of pressing global issues, including the United States’ unwavering support for a democratic, sovereign, and prosperous Ukraine. https://t.co/9mb9FbMm7M

.@SecBlinken discusses working to end global dependence on Russian energy and a new Strategic Concept to ensure that @NATO is prepared to face emerging threats over the next decade. https://t.co/XYGwF6na1b
.@SecBlinken: We’re raising the costs on Russia to bring the war to an end more swiftly through unprecedented sanctions and export controls. https://t.co/cpmSsa0ax1
.@SecBlinken: President Putin’s objective, in his own words, was to eliminate Ukraine as a sovereign, independent country. That effort has failed. https://t.co/Gku1ckb9sV

.@POTUS addressing food shortages caused by Russian aggression: Putin’s war, not sanctions, are impacting the harvest of food and disrupting the movement of that food by land and sea to nations around the globe that need it. https://t.co/gJyBX2lzZB

This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX ​and the African Vaccine Acquisition Trust #AVAT to donate more than more 3.2 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to Mali, Nigeria, Seychelles, and Tanzania. Learn more about our vaccine donation efforts: https://t.co/meu9mc1nm5 https://t.co/M3nFSM3egK

This week, in partnership with #COVAX, the United States donated 657,540 doses of the COVID-19 vaccine to the people of Nicaragua. With this donation, the U.S. reconfirms its commitment to the health and safety of the Nicaraguan people. https://t.co/H1sly3jTuL

The global food security crisis left 193 million people in need of urgent assistance in 2021, and it is getting worse due to COVID-19, widespread supply chain disruptions, the climate crisis, and ongoing conflict. https://t.co/4JjSwhukdX https://t.co/P5RZzsbx4D
The health and wellbeing of our ocean affects us all. The world must come together to protect it. #OnePlanetOneOcean
President Putin’s unprovoked war of choice in Ukraine is costing lives, destroying farms and livelihoods, and exacerbating a global food crisis. Read more from @ShareAmerica: https://t.co/vX1aLZYF0s
.@SecBlinken discusses the spike in the global food crisis: “Russia has destroyed Ukraine’s agricultural infrastructure.” https://t.co/VNGFCVL8ae

.@SecBlinken: The @WorldBank calculates that Russia’s unprovoked war on Ukraine, long one of the world’s bread baskets, will add at least another 40 million people to the ranks of those who are severely food insecure. Full remarks: https://t.co/xyK9gCmqQo https://t.co/dUNSmYn7Et

Food security means having enough safe and nutritious food to support an active, healthy life. The current food crisis can destabilize societies, increase hunger & malnutrition, drive migration and conflict, & cause severe economic dislocation. Learn more: https://t.co/4JjSwhukdX
Live now! @SecBlinken holds a press availability in Berlin, Germany. https://t.co/wbLW0RLfK8
Today at approximately 11 a.m. EDT, @SecBlinken will hold a press availability in Berlin, Germany. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lZRv7 and https://t.co/aDIlvOh5pD.

Today, @SecBlinken will participate in the International Ministerial Conference "Uniting for Global Food Security" in Berlin, Germany. Watch the conference live at 8:00 AM EDT. https://t.co/EFICuMpVpX

By sharing COVID-19 vaccines, the U.S. is creating a safer, more secure world for everyone. Yesterday we delivered 4,800 pediatric COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of Kosovo. These lifesaving doses are part of the 5​60 million doses the U.S. has already shared with the world. https://t.co/OhJuePllXi

Today we celebrate:
🏀 50 years of #TitleIX
⚽️ 10 years of the State Department and @espnW's @SportsEmpower Program #GSMP10
🎾 1 bright future for women and girls around the globe
#SportsDiplomacy https://t.co/ZNhWjtB6xx

The Kremlin is weaponizing food and endangering the world's access to critical food resources. Today, newly declassified intelligence suggests that Russian forces are destroying Ukrainian grain terminals and silos, including an attack on Ukraine's second largest terminal. https://t.co/FoxbOKtEOn

.@POTUS addressing food shortages caused by Russian aggression: Putin’s war, not sanctions, are impacting the harvest of food and disrupting the movement of that food by land and sea to nations around the globe that need it. https://t.co/t8ManU94ka

.@SecBlinken: Sanctions aren’t blocking Black Sea ports, trapping ships filled with food, and destroying Ukrainian roads and railways; Russia is. Sanctions are not emptying Ukrainian grain silos and stealing Ukrainian farm equipment; Russia is. https://t.co/XuzuraREuh

Reliable news sources reveal the reality of Russia’s war on Ukraine, including a food security crisis that especially impacts those in the poorest parts of the world. The United States will continue to work with our allies and partners to hold the Kremlin accountable. https://t.co/OkMLJYI8TA

It is the Kremlin’s unprovoked war that is exacerbating the global food crisis — not the sanctions. Instead of spreading disinformation and lies, Russia should allow Ukraine to ship its grain. Learn more: https://t.co/PGIwRbjLYT https://t.co/s6s4OI0rRY

Today we celebrate 50 years of #TitleIX—a landmark law that has provided new opportunities in education and sports for women and girls across the United States. Through our #SportsDiplomacy and other programs, the Department aims to promote gender equality around the world. https://t.co/A7N5fulFJM

During today’s briefing, @StateDeptSpox discussed the @ECAatState event today to mark the upcoming 50th anniversary of #TitleIX and also recognized Collin Walsh on being named CAREERS and the disABLED magazine’s 2022 employee of the year. More: https://t.co/l827DUraPq
.@SecBlinken gives background on the founding of @glifaa as they celebrate their 30th anniversary. #PrideMonth https://t.co/J0ttY07iac

The State Department and @espnW celebrate 10 years of @SportsEmpower, a program that strengthens society by empowering women and girls through sport. The GSMP has alumnae in 67 countries and has directly impacted over 350,000 people around the world. https://t.co/BSQwzPPGOI

.@SecBlinken on standing up for human rights around the world and ensuring that LGBTQI+ persons are treated with respect and humanity: “We have to continue—and we will continue—to stand up for equal rights everywhere, in partnership with local activists and local communities.” https://t.co/34NUk8uLL9

It is President Putin’s war of choice that is exacerbating the global food crisis—not sanctions, as he claims. Disinformation can't hide the Kremlin's responsibility. Russia should stop weaponizing food. Learn more: https://t.co/PGIwRbBmQr
FACT: President Putin’s brutal and needless war of choice has caused a humanitarian catastrophe in Ukraine. https://t.co/nBNQTyWDxe https://t.co/8qUI8SYC9E

The work of the U.S.-EU Trade and Technology Council (TTC) is important — but also can be complicated. In our latest explainer video, @State_E Under Secretary Jose Fernandez shares how it impacts your life. https://t.co/Jvp0FOxzKT https://t.co/QnTGkn23iW

On Wednesday, June 29 at 9 a.m. EDT join us for a conversation on @StateDeptDEIA and other Department efforts to advance LGBTQI+ equity within our institution and in our foreign policy. More: https://t.co/MhXbqzfdyg. https://t.co/An6EuXCF1R

Last week the United States delivered over 2.2 million pediatric COVID-19 vaccine doses to Nepal, in partnership with #COVAX. Our joint vaccination efforts with the people of Nepal help protect children and communities from harmful COVID-19 outcomes. https://t.co/PYfBqp4zxH

Last week @POTUS signed an executive order to advance LGBTQI+ equality at home & abroad. @SecBlinken: “With support from Congress, from other countries, private sector partners, we have provided more than $100 million to LGBTQI+ organizations in more than 100 countries.” #Pride https://t.co/7MAcWvaKIi

.@SecBlinken message to @CBYXPPP on their impact on 🇺🇸-🇩🇪relations: This year you —  each and every one of you — has planted new seeds of friendship between our countries. We’re already seeing them start to blossom. @ECAatState @Bundestag https://t.co/YfLkxr85nm

.@SecBlinken on the importance of cultural exchanges: It really is how we get new points of views, how we learn to see the world through the eyes of people with different histories, different cultures, different perspectives. @CBYXPPP @ECAatState @Bundestag https://t.co/MVkwxt8veA

.@StateDeptSpox reads out Attorney General Merrick Garland and @StateDept_GCJ's visit to the Ukraine-Poland border in support of efforts to hold accountable those responsible for war crimes and other atrocities during Russia’s unprovoked and brutal war against Ukraine. https://t.co/SlPpCZB6aF

.@StateDeptSpox on elections in Colombia: The U.S. congratulates the Colombian people for holding a free and fair presidential election on June 19. We look forward to working with President-Elect @PetroGustavo and his new administration & to continuing our strong collaboration. https://t.co/KEp5QGl5nL
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/dyrGgSft80. https://t.co/0o9EZJszk2

Before the Kremlin’s further invasion of Ukraine, 8 years of aggression resulted in 14,000 dead and over 1.4 million internally displaced. Russia’s ongoing war has since displaced and made refugees of millions more Ukrainian citizens. #StoptheLies More: https://t.co/q1GlXElpaX https://t.co/wzGkF3lwN8
Live now! @SecBlinken hosts the Congress-Bundestag Youth Exchange (CBYX) Welcome Home Event at the State Department. Also streaming at https://t.co/goNFH3BJLJ. https://t.co/64iZFMHLVA

We invite you to participate in “19 Days Celebrating Juneteenth with Step Afrika!": a worldwide event featuring virtual performances by the award-winning African American dance company and @CultureAtState alum, @StepAfrikaHQ. https://t.co/kBzl0DTS23

.@PRMAsstSec: This year's #WorldRefugeeDay comes at a time of great upheaval and many demands—from the response to the war in Ukraine, to the continuing efforts to resettle Afghan allies, to migration challenges in the Americas, to the influx of Congolese refugees into Uganda.

As the largest donor of humanitarian assistance, the United States remains committed to helping the world’s most vulnerable—including refugees around the world. #WorldRefugeeDay @StatePRM https://t.co/flV2lYC2mQ

This weekend, the United States delivered over 300,000 pediatric doses of Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Mongolia through COVAX. We are proud to be Mongolia’s #ThirdNeighbor and will continue to support the health and well-being of Mongolian communities. https://t.co/3ifX1aOjpz

This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to donate 3 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of Ghana, Mali, and Uganda. In total, the U.S. has now delivered more than 550 million vaccine doses to over 115 countries. https://t.co/R6H3xjno60 https://t.co/Q7EgQcEfJs
.@SecBlinken: We must sustain the fight against systemic racism and oppression every day. https://t.co/yqu95F5sFE

In 2021, @POTUS proclaimed #Juneteenth as a federal holiday to commemorate the end of slavery in America more than 150 years ago and the work that is still being done. Read the latest @ShareAmerica article on how cities across the U.S. observe Juneteenth: https://t.co/FV2m7zCUI8
This #Juneteenth, @CultureAtState has partnered with the talented dancers of @StepAfrika to create a 4-part video series highlighting African American history, traditions, and trailblazers.

On this day in 1865, enslaved African Americans of Galveston received word of their freedom, which had been purposefully withheld for 2+ years after the Emancipation Proclamation. On #Juneteenth, we continue to fight against systemic racism and build a freer, stronger country. https://t.co/9oQNPuvwAz

#Juneteenth is an important day in American history and, as of last year, is also a U.S. federal holiday. Today, we honor the contributions, strength, and resilience of the Black community and their ancestors. https://t.co/APnBU6XEv0
Happy Father’s Day! Today we honor fathers around the world who are teaching and shaping the next generation of world leaders. https://t.co/Eryz5Slujq

Today on International Day for the Elimination of Sexual Violence in Conflict, we stand in solidarity with survivors and those working to support them. The U.S. remains committed to preventing and responding to conflict-related sexual violence. https://t.co/hzAnYFPHJr

.@SecBlinken: The United States continues to be intently focused on fighting the COVID-19 pandemic and leaving the world better prepared and better defended for whatever comes next. https://t.co/YdbxNbccVJ
Sign up to receive "From the Desk of Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken," email updates from the Secretary on key foreign affairs issues. https://t.co/Rgm7xA0z8l https://t.co/WfKOlwxmTp

.@SecBlinken: The Biden administration has worked to re-energize America’s unmatched network of alliances and partnerships and to re-engage in international institutions. We’re encouraging partners to work with each other, and through regional and global organizations. https://t.co/qpuTGVhgj7

This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to donate 3 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of Ghana, Mali, and Uganda. In total, the U.S. has now delivered more than 550 million vaccine doses to over 115 countries. https://t.co/R6H3xj5MHq https://t.co/VM04lidJOA
Live now! @SecBlinken hosts and delivers remarks at a #Pride Reception at the State Department. Also streaming at https://t.co/TIfm85q3ir.  https://t.co/UFKb9QrDTU
Get the details here in the first ever report on whole-of-government efforts to advance LGBTQI+ human rights: https://t.co/jIBBTFE3oW

We proudly work every day to: 
✅combat criminalization 
✅protect LGBTQI+ refugees & asylum seekers 
✅provide foreign assistance to support LGBTQI+ persons 
✅respond to human rights abuses against LGBTQI+ persons 
✅build coalitions in the fight against LGBTQI+ discrimination

This #Pride Month, the United States reemphasizes our commitment to work with governments around the globe and civil society advocates to advance the human rights of LGBTQI+ persons. https://t.co/0w7IYgkGeI

Societies are strongest when all people are included and valued. Our new Special Representative for Racial Equity and Justice, Desirée Cormier Smith, will help ensure that racial equity and support for underserved communities remains central to our foreign policy. https://t.co/oshONqHKkz

We are excited to announce that on June 22, @FLOTUS and @BillieJeanKing will headline a celebration of #TitleIX's 50th anniversary and the 10th anniversary of the State Department and @espnW's @SportsEmpower Program. #SportsDiplomacy #GSMP10 https://t.co/0w4o9mIK10 https://t.co/mKEwibTBQk
Live now! @SecBlinken hosts and delivers remarks at a Diplomatic Corps Reception at the State Department. Also streaming at https://t.co/ag20IwFm3C. https://t.co/FixTSPnN8C

In the latest DipNote blog by @StateISN, Ambassador Scheinman explains how Russia's invasion of Ukraine goes against the central aims of the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty and why the NPT remains a critical tool in today's changing world. https://t.co/BQ9DPQiJkR
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/aO467uuKt9. https://t.co/XJCaiH2Hld

Today is #WorldSeaTurtleDay! Six of the world’s seven species of marine turtles are listed as endangered or threatened under the Endangered Species Act. The U.S. works to protect sea turtles around the world through its Section 609 certification program. https://t.co/bKzas1Bu7X https://t.co/2OY2gj1gd3

.@POTUS: Democracies are rising to meet this moment, rallying the world to the side of peace and the side of security. We’re showing our strength, and we will not falter. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/uy3H8cJhNf

.@StateDept_GCJ on efforts to hold the Kremlin accountable: We are working with civil society actors and others to document abuses for future accountability purposes. We are supporting the office of the prosecutor general in her efforts to prepare war crimes files and cases.

.@StateDept_GCJ at the @ForeignPressCtr: “It’s now been more than a hundred days since Russia relaunched its war of aggression with Ukraine…We’ve seen and have determined that a number of war crimes have been committed by Russia’s forces.” More: https://t.co/gSntgPiRvp

200 years of U.S.-Colombia relations wouldn't be possible without the dedicated work of staff @USEmbassyBogota, building ties between our nations. In our latest DipNote, check out how #200AñosDeAmistad has also been a personal journey for one of our staff. https://t.co/V5PrlbrPus

.@SecBlinken: The United States continues to be intently focused on fighting the COVID-19 pandemic and leaving the world better prepared and better defended for whatever comes next. https://t.co/UU59jgU1i6

LGBTQI+ status or conduct is criminalized in over 70 countries. The United States advocates for and works to empower LGBTQI+ human rights defenders seeking respect for human rights and an end to criminalization. Here are some ways we help: #Pride2022 https://t.co/Lz9JPWgg7C.

Clean energy is our future, and building the technology will require critical minerals. We’re working to bolster supply chains to ensure a more sustainable future. The newly established Minerals Security Partnership aims to do just that.

On June 19, 1822, Colombia and the United States officially established diplomatic relations. Since then, the two countries have shared a deep partnership based on mutual respect and a commitment to a common future. We celebrate #200YearsOfFriendship and look forward to 200 more.
.@SecBlinken: The United States, in partnership with #COVAX, will begin donating pediatric Pfizer vaccines for 5 to 12-year-olds. https://t.co/OlJsootI6o
Live now! @SecBlinken delivers remarks at the COVID-19 Global Action Plan Meeting at the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/lZeSVsRKgD. https://t.co/WJWtLFXbsj

The United States works closely with Colombia to investigate, arrest, and prosecute transnational criminal organizations and terrorist groups whose activities hurt citizens of both countries. https://t.co/eQ5t0X4oxD
Colombia is a vital strategic partner, and the United States is proud to stand with the Colombian people as they continue on the path to lasting peace and prosperity. https://t.co/eENzp2ivpD

Colombia is a strong supporter of bilingual education and academic mobility initiatives and the largest foreign government sponsor of the Fulbright program. https://t.co/liJsOhlUj3 https://t.co/SAHjOPXuDZ

This week, the United States and Colombia celebrate 200 years of diplomatic relations! The United States and Colombia share a commitment to promoting security, prosperity, and democratic governance in Colombia and across the Western Hemisphere. https://t.co/V7t60v7fRp https://t.co/lqDuZfUCTI

.@SecBlinken: President Putin is stopping food from being shipped and aggressively using his propaganda machine to deflect or distort responsibility because he hopes it will get the world to give in to him and end the sanctions. In other words, quite simply put, it’s blackmail. https://t.co/560SG55gGp
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/G7RO6T7GMK. https://t.co/SOedKn9BJ6

A more diverse, equitable, inclusive, and accessible Department leads to a stronger, smarter, and more effective workforce to handle the foreign policy challenges of the 21st Century. Follow our new @StateDeptDEIA office to learn more about the Department’s #DEIA efforts.

Today, we celebrate 247 years of the @USArmy! We salute all those who demonstrate their courage and patriotism every day while serving, defending, and protecting America around the world. #ArmyBDay https://t.co/pXKv9lvi6F

Today on #FlagDay we recognize the symbol of freedom, hope, and opportunity that the American flag represents—and honor those who have sacrificed and protected those values for the past 240 years. https://t.co/nmNTHi1ET2 https://t.co/qQ817hqKof

FACT: The Kremlin's unprovoked war in Ukraine is accelerating the global food security crisis. The United States is taking action and has pledged $2.6 billion in humanitarian food assistance to help alleviate hunger, with an added $5 billion over the next five years. https://t.co/F7zcOXpmgq

.@SecBlinken on the United States and Republic of Korea: We are standing together on global security challenges, including President Putin’s unprovoked war on Ukraine. We’re working together to promote peace, stability, and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific and beyond. https://t.co/6zGMoVNfWJ

.@SecBlinken: The United States has absolutely no hostile intent toward the DPRK. We’re open to dialogue without preconditions. We want to support the people of North Korea, including with COVID-19 vaccines. Our goal, simply put, is a peaceful and stable region and world. https://t.co/Jhj9WcgDX6

The United States continues to lead the global fight against the COVID-19 pandemic because no one is safe until everyone is safe. Today, we’re proud to announce we’ve reached another milestone, donating over 550 million safe and effective vaccine doses to the world. https://t.co/AGCkGAnTUV
Live now! @SecBlinken holds a joint press availability with Republic of Korea Foreign Minister @FMParkJin. Also streaming live at https://t.co/RyTBP7TCR5. https://t.co/ZggrjfsArG
.@SecBlinken: We can’t wait for President Putin to do the right thing.  The rest of the world must urgently and collectively respond to the emergency. https://t.co/fRxEAHS9yF

This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to donate more than 1.2 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to Angola, Benin, Guinea, and Lesotho. Where else have we donated COVID-19 vaccines? Check out our interactive map to learn more: https://t.co/LI3DDDnh5a https://t.co/071xKsF3ic

.@SecBlinken: Our task is to prove once again that democracy can meet urgent challenges, create opportunity, advance human dignity; that the future belongs to those who believe in freedom—and that all countries will be free to chart their own paths without coercion. https://t.co/09PwfKMQww

.@SecBlinken: “We don’t seek to block China from its role as a major power, nor to stop China—or any other country, for that matter—from growing their economy or advancing the interests of their people.” Read or watch the full speech: https://t.co/ASE2VEGobF
